subtitle.en: Clock time, time prepositions, daily routines, and making social plans in Belgian Dutch.

milestone.en: Can tell the time (including the 'half drie = 2:30' trap), use om / op / in correctly, say what you like to do, and invite / accept / decline plans with someone.

reference.note: If a leerpad rule mentions 'persoonsvorm' or 'onderwerp' and you blank, this is the cheat sheet.

vocabulary.note: en / maar / want / omdat — the glue for longer sentences.
nooit / soms / dikwijls / meestal / altijd — pairs well with 'graag' patterns.

grammar.note: Main clauses vs subclauses. The omdat-clause-verb-at-end rule comes up constantly.
Introduce the five modals. Module 5 is where you'll drill modal + infinitive at the end.
'afspreken' is separable — see the rule once so the split makes sense.

tests.note: Run this twice — the om/op/in distinction is a long-term retention problem.
